text:
Moswana (モスワナ, Mosuwana) is the Private Guard of the Second Prince Camilla in charge of killing the First Prince Benjamin through a Nen curse. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Appearance

Moswana has light-colored irises and shoulder-length fair hair that she combs behind her ears, except for a shorter lock that falls on her forehead. She has arched eyebrows, a prominent nose with a down-turned tip, and a mole between the right corner of her lips and her jawline. She wears the standard uniform of Camilla's private guards. 

Personality

Like the other Have-Nots in Camilla's employ, Moswana is fiercely loyal to her benefactor, to the point of being eager to die so she can be of use to her, which she regards as the reason she was born. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Moswanna stood close by to Sarahell and her Prince, Camilla, who complained that she didn't want the Royal Bodyguard Benjamin sent over anywhere close to her and demands to see her mummy. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 

After Prince Camilla's first hearing before Cleapatro, Moswana and Nukuocon escort her to the VVIP area. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 A few days later, while Sarahell speaks to the other bodyguards in the hall of the room, Moswana stands guard in the prince's bedroom, eager to die and curse Benjamin. 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Moswana is a Nen user and can cast the same curse as the other Have-Nots in Prince Camilla's employ. 

Manga Appearances

Quotes

"Sigh... I want to die!" 

Trivia

It is unclear if Moswana was the guard who defended Camilla in Chapter 376. Since the attorney's features notably differed from Moswana's and Sarahell's design had already been shown in Chapter 373, the information is included in Nukuocon's page.
The furigana name of the Have-Nots' curse is derived from Japanese mythology, specifically to Izanami's inability to leave the underworld due to having eaten of its food.
The kanji name of the curse, "Tsujitsuma Awase ni Umareta Bokura", is derived from a song by Amazarashi.
Viz localized the curse's name as "Dust in the Wind: Hell Fruit".
